summary: TEKOSECURE FASE 2 — Real actions (SSH Mikrotik + Fernet + DRY_RUN + local audit fallback + Block IP modal). Backend: 16/16 pytest pass (8 new tests for phase 2 + all phase 1 regression). Frontend: mode badge orange DRY_RUN visible, 4 BLOQUEAR IP buttons on active alerts, modal opens with correct IP/attack/severity + warning box, cancel & confirm work, POST /api/actions/block-ip-real returns success with locations_blocked=[MATRIZ_KM6,OASIS,KM12,HERNANDARIAS] mode=DRY_RUN expires=24h, toast success shown. Local audit.log fallback verified (5 entries written since Supabase RLS blocks JWT inserts — expected).
action_items: KNOWN DEBT (expected): actions_log Supabase table has restrictive RLS blocking INSERT from user JWT — fallback to /app/backend/logs/audit.log works. Run /app/scripts/create_actions_log.sql in Supabase to enable proper policies so /api/actions/audit-log and /api/actions/blocked-ips can return entries from DB.

Seed alerts partially consumed by earlier phase 1 test (test_close_alert_flow still closes one active alert). Re-seed via 'python3 /app/scripts/seed_supabase.py' before demo if needed. Currently 4 ACTIVE alerts remain (was 5 before this run — one closed by regression test).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py v2 (~370 lines) is well structured, uses env vars, async httpx, Pydantic; audit fallback is robust and never crashes the endpoint on Supabase errors.
config_secure.py cleanly separates non-secret config (json) from Fernet-encrypted password. Good use of SecretsError.
mikrotik_actions.py has clean DRY_RUN branching in connect() and _exec() — no side effects when dry_run=True.
BlockIpModal.jsx correctly obtains fresh Supabase session token via getSession() before POST — good.
MikrotikActionManager.connect_all is sequential; for 4 devices this is fine (<4s) but could be moved to ThreadPoolExecutor when scale grows.
Minor: /api/actions/blocked-ips returns success=false when Supabase 4xx; UI should treat this as empty list not error.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (added 8 Fase 2 test functions + fixed BASE_URL default)
seed_data_creation: None. Regression test closes 1 active alert; re-seed if needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 2 works end-to-end in DRY_RUN. To validate REAL mode (MIKROTIK_DRY_RUN=false), the pod would need LAN access to 192.168.13.100/192.168.12.244/etc — NOT possible from the Emergent preview. To exercise audit-log DB path (not fallback), first run /app/scripts/create_actions_log.sql in Supabase SQL editor. Backend logs at /app/backend/logs/api.log and audit at /app/backend/logs/audit.log. All 16 backend pytest tests idempotent except test_close_alert_flow which mutates one ACTIVE alert per run.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs. Supabase RLS blocking audit inserts is expected & documented; fallback to file works.
